author: agent-overnight
mechanism_text: Sanity-check trial: trailing 12-month country return should positively predict next-month country return via the well-documented cross-sectional momentum effect. This is a harness validation run; the known literature effect should produce a modestly positive IC, confirming the pipeline end to end.

note: LEAK: 12MRet is a forward return labeled at window start (optimizer target). Verdict invalid; variable now blacklisted in harness. Trial still counts.

author: agent-overnight
mechanism_text: Sanity-check trial 2: trailing 12-minus-1-month country total return (12-1MTR_CS, cross-sectionally normalized, trailing by construction - verified corr +0.81 with raw trailing 12-1) should positively predict next-month country returns via the documented cross-sectional momentum effect. Expect modest IC around 0.02-0.06.

author: agent-overnight
mechanism_text: Trade-weighted neighbor 21d return minus own 21d return should positively predict the country forward return: when a country trade network has repriced and the country itself has not, the endpoint catches up as the shock propagates through trade channels slower than prices move at the network center. Unconditional daily cross-sectional test of the v1 graph feature.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Classic carry-premium mechanism applied to country equities: countries whose currencies price high forward-implied carry versus USD offer compensation for devaluation and sudden-stop risk; in normal regimes that risk premium is harvested, so high-carry countries should outperform low-carry countries cross-sectionally over the next weeks, with known crash risk in risk-off episodes that the sub-period table should expose.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Change-in-carry variant of the carry hypothesis: a carry level that is high versus the country's own trailing year means forwards have recently moved to price more depreciation or higher local rates; if that move overshoots actual macro deterioration, the country's equity market earns an elevated risk premium afterwards, so a high carry z-score should predict relative outperformance.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: The one-week minus three-month implied vol slope inverts positive only when the options market prices an imminent event; countries whose near-term vol is unusually elevated versus their own history face event risk that equity prices have historically been slow to fully discount, so a stretched positive term slope should predict near-term underperformance and a calm slope outperformance.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: The 25-delta butterfly measures the premium paid for BOTH tails of the currency distribution; a butterfly rich versus its own trailing year means the options market is buying crash-and-spike protection, which historically precedes realized currency stress, and currency stress passes through to equity underperformance in the following weeks for that country.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: The 25-delta risk reversal is the cleanest daily price of devaluation fear; a risk reversal stretched versus its own trailing year means crash protection is being bid, and if equity prices have not yet reflected that repricing the country should underperform over the following days to weeks, which is exactly the disagreement detector D10 trades on and is here tested as a standalone rank.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Distress-risk premium mechanism: a CDS curve flat or inverted versus its own history marks a country priced for imminent trouble, and the distressed-asset literature plus our 2026-06-12 cds_inversion event study (post-onset equity outperformance of roughly four percent over a quarter) suggest such pricing overshoots, so LOW slope z should predict relative equity outperformance as the panic premium decays.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: A government curve steep versus its own trailing year reflects easing expectations or recovering growth pricing while a flat or inverted curve reflects tightening into slowdown; the recession-predictor literature says equity markets under-react to curve information at the country level, so a high 2s10s z-score should predict relative outperformance over weeks to months.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Post-announcement drift mechanism at the macro level: when a country's growth data (GDP, PMI, falling unemployment) beats the economist survey median, the full implication for earnings and policy is incorporated only gradually, so a positive composite growth surprise should predict continued relative equity outperformance over the following one to three months, the macro analogue of post-earnings-announcement drift.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Hot inflation surprises force monetary policy tighter than the market had priced and compress equity multiples with a lag; cool surprises do the reverse, so a country printing inflation above the survey median should underperform peers over the following months while a country printing below should outperform, making LOW inflation surprise z the favorable rank.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Re-test of H_20260612_003 on the 26 countries whose options surfaces have stable history since 2008 (round 1 failed the coverage gate from a mis-declared 34-country universe, not from the data): a 1W-minus-3M implied vol slope stretched positive means the options market prices an imminent event, and equities historically under-discount that near-term event risk, so calm term slopes should outperform stretched ones.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Re-test of H_20260612_004 on the stable 26-country options universe (round 1 coverage-gated by a mis-declared universe while showing IC t-stat near 2.6): a 25-delta butterfly rich versus its own trailing year means both currency tails are being bought, which precedes realized currency stress that passes through to country equity underperformance over the following weeks.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Re-test of H_20260612_005 on the stable 26-country options universe after the round 1 coverage gate: a risk reversal stretched versus its own trailing year means devaluation protection is being bid, and where equity prices have not yet repriced that fear the country should underperform peers over the following days to weeks, the standalone-rank version of detector D10.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Re-test of H_20260612_007 on the 20 countries with stable daily 2Y and 10Y generics since 2008 (round 1 missed the coverage gate at 94 percent on a 27-country declaration while showing IC t-stat near 2.7): a government curve steep versus its own trailing year reflects easing or recovery expectations that country equity markets incorporate only gradually, so high 2s10s z should predict relative outperformance over weeks to months.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: Re-test of H_20260612_008 on the 14 countries whose composite growth surprise has stable monthly coverage since 2008: when growth data beats the economist survey median the earnings and policy implications are priced only gradually, so positive composite growth surprises should predict continued relative outperformance over one to three months, macro post-announcement drift.

author: agent_sweep_2026_06_12
mechanism_text: FLIPPED re-registration of H_20260612_009, which was DEAD as registered but carried IC t-stat of plus 2.99 in the opposite direction - this is trial two of the idea and is charged to the family accordingly. Proposed mechanism for the reversal: at the country level upside inflation surprises are dominated by demand strength and commodity-terms-of-trade booms (reflation), which lift nominal earnings and equity returns over the following months, outweighing the discount-rate drag that the original disinflation hypothesis assumed.
